Groupon Inc.。创始人安德鲁·梅森(Andrew Mason)周四被解雇后，向员工发送了一份备忘录。以下是他的信的正文：
在担任Groupon首席执行官四年半之后，我决定花更多的时间和家人在一起。开玩笑的-我今天被解雇了。如果你想知道为什么..。你一直没有注意到这一点。从S1中有争议的指标，到我们的实质性疲软，再到两个季度低于我们自己的预期，以及股价徘徊在上市价格的四分之一左右，过去一年半的事件不言而喻。作为首席执行官，我是负责任的。
你在Groupon做了令人惊叹的事情，你值得外部世界给你第二次机会。我正在妨碍这一点。一位新的首席执行官为你赢得了这个机会。董事会一致支持我们在过去几个月里分享的战略，我从未见过你们作为一家全球性公司如此有效地合作--现在是时候让Groupon从公众的噪音中解脱出来了。
对于那些关心我的人，请不要这样--我爱Groupon，我为我们创造的一切感到非常自豪。这段旅程失败了，我觉得没什么。如果Groupon是战车，那就好像我在第一次比赛中不会死掉就能一路走到Terra Tubes。我很幸运能有机会和你们一起带领公司走到这一步。我现在会花一些时间来减压(仅供参考，如果有人有建议的话，我正在寻找一个好的减肥夏令营来减掉我的Groupon 40)，然后也许我会想出如何将这种体验转化为有成效的东西。
如果这位简单的朝圣者想要传授给你一条智慧的话，那就是：鼓起勇气从顾客开始。我最大的遗憾是，我让数据的缺乏压倒了我关于什么对我们的客户最好的直觉。这一领导层的更迭给了你一些喘息的空间，让你改掉坏习惯，带来可持续的客户快乐--不要浪费这个机会！
note_en:
Groupon Inc. founder Andrew Mason sent a memo to employees  after he was fired Thursday. Here's the text of his letter:
  After four and a half intense and wonderful years as CEO of Groupon, I've decided that I'd like to spend more time with my family. Just kidding – I was fired today. If you're wondering why ... you haven't been paying attention. From controversial metrics in our S1 to our material weakness to two quarters of missing our own expectations and a stock price that's hovering around one quarter of our listing price, the events of the last year and a half speak for themselves. As CEO, I am accountable.
 You are doing amazing things at Groupon, and you deserve the outside world to give you a second chance. I'm getting in the way of that. A fresh CEO earns you that chance. The board is aligned behind the strategy we've shared over the last few months, and I've never seen you working together more effectively as a global company – it's time to give Groupon a relief valve from the public noise.
 For those who are concerned about me, please don't be – I love Groupon, and I'm terribly proud of what we've created. I'm OK with having failed at this part of the journey. If Groupon was Battletoads, it would be like I made it all the way to the Terra Tubes without dying on my first ever play through. I am so lucky to have had the opportunity to take the company this far with all of you. I'll now take some time to decompress (FYI I'm looking for a good fat camp to lose my Groupon 40, if anyone has a suggestion), and then maybe I'll figure out how to channel this experience into something productive.
 If there's one piece of wisdom that this simple pilgrim would like to impart upon you: have the courage to start with the customer. My biggest regrets are the moments that I let a lack of data override my intuition on what's best for our customers. This leadership change gives you some breathing room to break bad habits and deliver sustainable customer happiness – don't waste the opportunity!
title: Groupon创始人安德鲁·梅森致员工的告别信(2013)
title_en: Groupon founder Andrew Mason's farewell letter to employees (2013)

跳到导航跳跃搜索堡垒或追踪意大利(这个短语不恰当地源自法语，字面意思是意大利的轮廓)，是一种在大炮主宰战场的早期现代火药时代演变出来的风格的防御工事。它最早出现在十五世纪中叶的意大利。有些类型，特别是当与沟壑和其他外墙结合在一起时，就像是同时代的相关星形堡垒。
堡垒的设计通常是一个多边形(需要引用)，在墙角有堡垒。这些露头消除了受保护的盲点，也就是所谓的死区，并允许从受到保护的位置沿窗帘开火，不受直接火力的影响。许多堡垒也以骑士队为特色，骑士队是完全基于主要结构的凸起的次要结构。
他们的前身是中世纪的堡垒，通常建在高山上。从那里，箭射向敌人。敌人们希望要么撞开大门，要么用梯子爬过墙来战胜防御者。对于侵略军来说，这些防御工事是很难攻克的，因此，堡垒在战争中占据了关键地位。
中世纪的被动环形防御工事被证明很容易受到炮火的破坏或摧毁，因为它可以从外面直接对准垂直的砖石墙。此外，一支可以接近城墙的进攻部队能够在相对安全的情况下进行破坏行动，因为防御者无法从附近的城墙向他们射击。相比之下，堡垒是一个非常平坦的结构，由许多专门设计成相互覆盖的三角形堡垒和一条沟渠组成。为了抵挡炮弹，防御墙变得更低、更厚。为了抵消较低的墙更容易攀登的事实，沟渠被加宽，以便进攻的步兵仍然暴露在更高海拔的火力下，包括从堡垒包围火力。沟渠的外侧通常设有冰川，以偏转瞄准主墙下部的炮弹。可以增加更多的结构，如沟壑、绳索、角工程或皇冠工程，甚至是独立的堡垒，以创造复杂的外部工程，以进一步保护主墙免受大炮的伤害，有时还提供额外的防御阵地。它们是由许多材料建造的，通常是土和砖，因为砖不会像石头那样在炮弹的撞击下破碎。[2]。
堡垒防御工事在十五世纪末和十六世纪初进一步发展，主要是为了应对法国对意大利半岛的入侵。法国军队装备了新的大炮和轰炸机，可以很容易地摧毁建于中世纪的传统防御工事。星形堡垒被米开朗基罗用在佛罗伦萨的防御性土方工程中，并在16世纪由Baldassare Peruzzi和Vincenzo Scamozzi改进。这种设计在15世纪30年代和15世纪40年代从意大利传播开来。
在接下来的三个世纪里，它在整个欧洲被大量使用。整个欧洲都非常需要意大利工程师来帮助建造新的防御工事。17世纪末的建筑师门诺·范·柯霍恩，特别是路易十四的军事工程师沃班，被认为将这种形式推向了逻辑的极端。要塞...。后备的沟壑和堡垒，帽檐和月牙帽，帽檐和帽檐，护卫物和皇冠，角器，曲线帽，丑角，悬崖，警戒线，长廊和扶手崖...&#34；[3]。
星形防御工事对文艺复兴时期理想城市的模式产生了重大影响：文艺复兴被一种城市类型所催眠，在一个半世纪的时间里--从费拉雷特到斯卡莫齐--对所有乌托邦的计划印象深刻：这就是星形城市。19世纪，爆炸性炮弹的发展改变了防御性防御工事的性质。葡萄牙的埃尔瓦斯被一些人认为是荷兰防御工事学派中幸存下来的最好的例子。
当新的有效的机动围攻炮在15世纪进入军事战略时，军事工程师的反应是安排将墙埋入土坡(冰川)前面的沟渠中，这样它们就不会受到破坏性的直接火力的攻击，并由吸收并在很大程度上消散俯冲火力的土堤覆盖在墙的顶部。在条件允许的地方，比如马耳他的马诺尔堡，沟渠被切入原生岩石中，沟渠内侧的墙壁完全是未经开采的原生岩石。随着围墙变得更低，他们也变得更容易受到攻击。
以前在炮塔设计中占主导地位的圆形形状创造了死区或死区，这些区域相对来说是防御火力的，因为墙的其他部分的直接火力不能直接射向弯曲的墙壁。为了防止这种情况，以前是圆形或方形的炮塔被延伸成菱形的尖端，使突袭步兵没有掩体。沟渠和围墙将进攻步兵引向精心建造的茨温格、贝利或类似的杀伤区，在那里，防御性大炮可以摧毁试图冲破城墙的步兵，并设置了阵地，使进攻步兵没有地方躲避防御性火力。
一个更深层次、更微妙的变化是从被动的防御模式转变为主动的防御模式。较低的墙壁更容易受到袭击，如果袭击者可以占领沟渠外部的斜坡，并在那里安装攻击大炮，那么土墙提供的抵御直接火力的保护就会失效。因此，这个形状的设计是为了最大限度地利用包围(或侧翼)火力来对付任何应该到达任何墙底部的攻击者。星光掩护的大炮上每个点底部的凹痕。这些大炮会在邻点的边缘有一条清晰的火线，而它们所在的星点则受到来自这些点底部的火力保护。这些想法的演变可以在意大利西北部的Sarzana等过渡性防御工事中看到。[5]。
因此，堡垒演变成复杂的形状，使得防御炮连可以指挥联锁的火场。前方连控制着斜坡，这些斜坡保卫着建筑群更深的墙壁，使其免受直接火力的袭击。防御大炮不仅仅是为了对付冲击城墙的企图，而是积极挑战进攻大炮，并阻止他们接近堡垒，直接向脆弱的城墙开火。
堡垒防御的钥匙移到了堡垒周围沟渠的外缘，也就是所谓的隐蔽通道。防御者可以在沟渠的掩护下相对安全地移动，并可以采取积极的对策来保持对冰川(位于沟渠外的开阔斜坡)的控制，方法是建造防御性的土方工程，阻止敌人进入冰川，从而阻止敌人直接进入城墙的射击点，并通过挖掘反地雷来拦截和破坏对要塞城墙的地雷企图。
与中世纪的防御工事相比，堡垒的面积变得更低、更大，提供了纵深的防御，攻击者需要克服多层防御，才能将大炮放在防御的内层。
用于防御大炮的发射阵地受到严密防御，不受外部炮火的轰炸，但向堡垒内部开放，不仅是为了在攻克它们时降低它们对攻击者的效用，而且还为了让防御大炮产生的大量烟雾消散。
这种类型的防御工事继续有效，而袭击者只配备了大炮，造成的大部分损害是由固体射击的撞击动量造成的。因为只有黑火药这样的低级炸药可用，爆炸炮弹对这种防御工事很大程度上是无效的。迫击炮和烈性炸药的发展，以及随之而来的爆炸性炮弹破坏力的大幅增加，从而使这种防御工事的复杂几何形状变得无关紧要。战争将变得更加机动。然而，花了很多年才摒弃了古老的堡垒思想。
堡垒非常昂贵。阿姆斯特丹的22个堡垒耗资1100万弗罗林，锡耶纳在1544年破产以支付防御费用。出于这个原因，堡垒通常是从早期的防御中临时搭建起来的。中世纪的幕墙被推倒，前面挖了一条沟。挖掘时使用的泥土被堆在墙后，形成一个坚固的结构。虽然专门建造的防御工事通常会有一个砖制的筋膜，因为这种材料能够吸收炮火的冲击，但许多临时防御通过省去这一阶段而选择更多的泥土来削减成本。即兴创作也可以包括降低中世纪的圆塔，并用泥土填满它们以加固结构。
还经常需要拓宽和加深城墙外的沟渠，以形成更有效的正面攻击和采矿障碍。15世纪20年代的工程师们还在沟渠前建造了巨大的、缓缓倾斜的土堤，称为冰川，这样墙壁几乎完全不受水平炮火的影响。冰川的主要好处是剥夺了敌军炮兵近距离射击的能力。仰角越小，拦截力越大。
意大利人痕迹的第一个关键实例是在教皇港口奇维塔维奇亚(Civitavecchia)，那里的原始墙壁被降低和加厚，因为石头在轰炸下往往会破碎。
第一次真正显示了TRACE ITALIENE的有效性的重大战斗是1500年比萨面对佛罗伦萨和法国联合军队的防御。随着最初的中世纪防御工事开始被法国的大炮炮火摧毁，比桑人在受到威胁的地区后面修建了一座土墙。人们发现，倾斜的土墙可以防御升级，而且比它取代的幕墙更能抵抗大炮的射击。
第二次围攻是1509年对帕多瓦的围攻。一位名叫弗拉·吉奥康多(Fra Giocondo)的和尚工程师受托保卫这座威尼斯人的城市，他推倒了这座城市的中世纪城墙，并用一条宽阔的沟渠包围了这座城市，这条沟渠可以被从炮口侧面发射的火力扫过，这些炮口被延伸到沟渠的突出部分设置得很低。法军和盟军发现炮火对这些低矮的城墙没有什么影响，于是进行了几次血腥和徒劳的攻击，然后撤退了。
这种新型的防御工事也在众多的地中海战争中发挥了作用，减缓了奥斯曼帝国的扩张。虽然罗兹在1480年被围困后部分升级为新型防御工事，但在1522年仍然被征服；然而，这是一场漫长而血腥的围困，被围困的人没有希望得到外部的救济，因为该岛靠近奥斯曼帝国的权力基地，远离任何盟友。另一方面，由于新的防御工事，奥斯曼帝国在1537年未能攻占科孚，跨越近两个世纪的几次尝试(另一次重大尝试是在1716年)也都失败了。[6][7]。
1552年，根据圣约翰的命令，在马耳他岛上修建了两座星级堡垒，即圣埃尔莫堡和圣米迦勒堡。圣埃尔莫堡在1565年的奥斯曼帝国围攻中发挥了关键作用，当时它设法坚持了一个多月的猛烈轰炸。最终它倒下了，但奥斯曼帝国的伤亡人数很高，这为从西西里赶来的救援部队争取了时间，以解救被围困的岛屿的其余部分。因此，星际堡垒在围攻中起到了至关重要的决定性作用。[8]。
威尼斯被拿破仑攻陷后，科孚于1797年被法国共和军占领。现在的古代防御工事在这一点上仍然有一定的价值。1799年，由乌沙科夫上将率领的俄罗斯-奥斯曼帝国-英国联盟和阿里·帕夏(Ali Pasha)派遣的军队在经历了四个月的围困后，夺回了科孚。当时，路易斯·弗朗索瓦·让·查伯特将军(General Louis François Jean Chabot)领导的驻军由于缺乏补给，失去了港口入口处的关键岛屿维多，投降了，并获准返回法国。[9][10]。
根据杰弗里·帕克(Geoffrey Parker)在他的文章“1560-1660年军事革命：一个神话？”中所述，早期现代欧洲出现了痕迹意大利，以及攻占这种防御工事的困难，导致了军事战略的深刻变化，帕克认为，最重要的是，攻击这些堡垒所需的军队规模增加了。帕克认为，战争变成了一系列旷日持久的围攻，在存在意大利痕迹的地区，露天战斗变得无关紧要。帕克认为，归根结底，军事地理学，换句话说，在特定地区是否存在微量的意大利，塑造了早期现代时期的军事战略。这是对迈克尔·罗伯茨1955年最初提出的军事革命命题的一次深刻变革。
帕克强调将防御工事作为关键要素，这引起了一些学者的强烈批评，如约翰·A·林恩和M·S·金格拉，特别是关于声称新堡垒设计与这一时期军队规模增加之间的因果联系。[11]。
在十九世纪，随着更强大的火炮和爆炸性炮弹的发展，星形堡垒被更简单但更坚固的多边形堡垒所取代。在20世纪，随着第一次世界大战期间和之后机动坦克和空战的发展，固定防御工事变得并不像前几个世纪那样重要。

Jump to navigation  Jump to search  A  bastion fort or   trace italienne (a phrase improperly derived from French, literally meaning  Italian outline), is a  fortification in a style that evolved during the  early modern period of  gunpowder when the  cannon came to dominate the  battlefield. It was first seen in the mid-fifteenth century in  Italy. Some types, especially when combined with  ravelins and other outworks, resembled the related  star fort of the same era.
 The design of the fort is normally a polygon [   citation needed] with  bastions at the corners of the walls. These outcroppings eliminated protected blind spots, called "dead zones", and allowed fire along the curtain from positions protected from direct fire. Many bastion forts also feature  cavaliers, which are raised secondary structures based entirely inside the primary structure.
    Their predecessors,  medieval fortresses, were usually placed on high  hills. From there,  arrows were shot at the enemies. The enemies' hope was to either ram the gate or climb over the wall with  ladders and overcome the defenders. For the invading force, these fortifications proved quite difficult to overcome, and accordingly, fortresses occupied a key position in warfare.
 Passive ring-shaped ( enceinte)  fortifications of the Medieval era proved vulnerable to damage or destruction by cannon fire, when it could be directed from outside against a perpendicular masonry wall. In addition, an attacking force that could get close to the wall was able to conduct undermining operations in relative safety, as the defenders could not shoot at them from nearby walls. In contrast, the bastion fortress was a very flat structure composed of many triangular  bastions, specifically designed to cover each other, and a ditch. In order to counteract the cannonballs, defensive walls were made lower and thicker. To counteract the fact that lower walls were easier to climb, the ditch was widened so that attacking infantry were still exposed to fire from a higher elevation, including enfilading fire from the bastions. The outer side of the ditch was usually provided with a  glacis to deflect cannonballs aimed at the lower part of the main wall. Further structures, such as  ravelins,  tenailles,  hornworks or  crownworks, and even detached forts could be added to create complex outer works to further protect the main wall from artillery, and sometimes provide additional defensive positions. They were built of many materials, usually earth and  brick, as brick does not shatter on impact from a cannonball as  stone does.  [2]
 Bastion fortifications were further developed in the late fifteenth and early sixteenth centuries primarily in response to the  French invasion of the Italian peninsula. The French army was equipped with new cannon and  bombards that were easily able to destroy traditional fortifications built in the  Middle Ages. Star forts were employed by  Michelangelo in the defensive earthworks of  Florence, and refined in the sixteenth century by  Baldassare Peruzzi and  Vincenzo Scamozzi. The design spread out of Italy in the 1530s and 1540s.
 It was employed heavily throughout Europe for the following three centuries. Italian engineers were heavily in demand throughout Europe to help build the new fortifications. The late-seventeenth-century architects  Menno van Coehoorn and especially  Vauban,  Louis XIV's military engineer, are considered to have taken the form to its logical extreme. "Fortresses... acquired  ravelins and  redoubts, bonnettes and  lunettes,  tenailles and tenaillons,  counterguards and  crownworks and  hornworks and curvettes and  fausse brayes and  scarps and cordons and  banquettes and  counterscarps..."  [3]
 The star-shaped fortification had a formative influence on the patterning of the Renaissance  ideal city: "The Renaissance was hypnotized by one city type which for a century and a half—from Filarete to Scamozzi—was impressed upon all utopian schemes: this is the star-shaped city."  [4] In the 19th century, the development of the  explosive shell changed the nature of defensive fortifications.  Elvas, in  Portugal is considered by some to be the best surviving example of the Dutch school of fortifications.
  When the newly-effective maneuverable siege cannon came into military strategy in the fifteenth century, the response from  military engineers was to arrange for the walls to be embedded into ditches fronted by earthen slopes ( glacis) so that they could not be attacked by destructive  direct fire and to have the walls topped by earthen banks that absorbed and largely dissipated the energy of  plunging fire. Where conditions allowed, as in  Fort Manoel in  Malta, the ditches were cut into the native rock, and the wall at the inside of the ditch was simply unquarried native rock. As the walls became lower, they also became more vulnerable to assault.
  The rounded shape that had previously been dominant for the design of turrets created "dead space", or "dead" zones, which were relatively sheltered from defending fire, because direct fire from other parts of the walls could not be directed around the curved wall. To prevent this, what had previously been round or square  turrets were extended into diamond-shaped points to give storming infantry no shelter. The ditches and walls channeled attacking infantry into carefully constructed  zwinger,  bailey, or similar " kill zone" grounds where defensive cannon could decimate infantry attempting to storm the walls, with emplacements set so that the attacking infantry had no place to shelter from the defensive fire.
  A further and more subtle change was to move from a passive model of defence to an active one. The lower walls were more vulnerable to being stormed, and the protection that the earthen banking provided against direct fire failed if the attackers could occupy the slope on the outside of the ditch and mount an attacking cannon there. Therefore, the shape was designed to make maximum use of   enfilade (or flanking) fire against any attackers who should reach the base of any of the walls. The indentations in the base of each point on the star sheltered cannons. Those cannons would have a clear line of fire directly down the edge of the neighboring points, while their point of the star was protected by fire from the base of those points. The evolution of these ideas can be seen in transitional fortifications such as  Sarzana in northwest Italy.  [5]
   Thus forts evolved complex shapes that allowed defensive batteries of cannon to command interlocking  fields of fire. Forward  batteries commanded the slopes which defended walls deeper in the complex from direct fire. The defending cannon were not simply intended to deal with attempts to storm the walls, but to actively challenge attacking cannon and deny them approach close enough to the fort to engage in direct fire against the vulnerable walls.
 The key to the fort's defence moved to the outer edge of the ditch surrounding the fort, known as the covered way, or covert way. Defenders could move relatively safely in the cover of the ditch and could engage in active countermeasures to keep control of the  glacis, the open slope that lay outside the ditch, by creating defensive earthworks to deny the enemy access to the glacis and thus to firing points that could bear directly onto the walls and by digging  counter mines to intercept and disrupt attempts to  mine the fort walls.
 Compared to  medieval fortifications, forts became both lower and larger in area, providing  defence in depth, with tiers of defences that an attacker needed to overcome in order to bring cannon to bear on the inner layers of defences.
 Firing emplacements for defending cannon were heavily defended from bombardment by external fire, but open towards the inside of the fort, not only to diminish their usefulness to the attacker should they be overcome, but also to allow the large volumes of smoke that the defending cannon would generate to dissipate.
 Fortifications of this type continued to be effective while the attackers were armed only with cannon, where the majority of the damage inflicted was caused by momentum from the impact of  solid shot. Because only low explosives such as black powder were available, explosive shells were largely ineffective against such fortifications. The development of  mortars,  high explosives, and the consequent large increase in the destructive power of  explosive shells and thus plunging fire rendered the intricate geometry of such fortifications irrelevant. Warfare was to become more mobile. It took, however, many years to abandon the old fortress thinking.
   Bastion forts were very expensive.  Amsterdam's 22 bastions cost 11 million florins, and  Siena in 1544 bankrupted itself to pay for its defences. For this reason, bastion forts were often improvised from earlier defences. Medieval curtain walls were torn down, and a ditch was dug in front of them. The earth used from the excavation was piled behind the walls to create a solid structure. While purpose-built fortifications would often have a brick fascia because of the material's ability to absorb the shock of artillery fire, many improvised defences cut costs by leaving this stage out and instead opting for more earth. Improvisation could also consist of lowering medieval round towers and infilling them with earth to strengthen the structures.
 It was also often necessary to widen and deepen the ditch outside the walls to create a more effective barrier to frontal assault and mining. Engineers from the 1520s were also building massive, gently sloping banks of earth called  glacis in front of ditches so that the walls were almost totally hidden from horizontal artillery fire. The main benefit of the glaces was to deny enemy artillery the ability to fire point blank. The lower the angle of elevation, the higher the stopping power.
    The first key instance of a  trace Italianate was at the  Papal port of  Civitavecchia, where the original walls were lowered and thickened because the stone tended to shatter under bombardment.
  The first major battle which truly showed the effectiveness of  trace Italienne was the defence of  Pisa in 1500 against a combined  Florentine and  French army. With the original medieval fortifications beginning to crumble to French cannon fire, the Pisans constructed an earthen  rampart behind the threatened sector. It was discovered that the sloping earthen rampart could be defended against  escalade and was also much more resistant to cannon fire than the  curtain wall it had replaced.
 The second siege was that of  Padua in 1509. A monk engineer named  Fra Giocondo, trusted with the defence of the  Venetian city, cut down the city's medieval wall and surrounded the city with a broad  ditch that could be swept by flanking fire from gun ports set low in projections extending into the ditch. Finding that their cannon fire made little impression on these low ramparts, the French and allied besiegers made several bloody and fruitless assaults and then withdrew.
 The new type of fortification also played a role in the numerous Mediterranean wars, slowing down the Ottoman expansion. Although  Rhodes had been partially upgraded to the new type of fortifications after the 1480 siege, it was still  conquered in 1522; nevertheless it was a long and bloody siege, and the besieged had no hope of outside relief because the island was close to the Ottoman power base and far from any allies. On the other hand, the Ottomans failed to take  Corfu in 1537 in no small part because of the new fortifications, and several attempts spanning almost two centuries (another major one was  in 1716) also failed.  [6]  [7]
  Two star forts were built by the  Order of Saint John on the island of  Malta in 1552,  Fort Saint Elmo and  Fort Saint Michael. Fort Saint Elmo played a critical role in the  Ottoman siege of 1565 when it managed to hold out heavy bombardment for over a month. Eventually it fell, but the Ottoman casualties were very high, and it bought time for the relief force which arrived from  Sicily to relieve the rest of the besieged island. The star fort therefore played a crucial and decisive role in the siege.  [8]
 After the fall of Venice to Napoleon, Corfu was occupied in 1797 by the French republican armies. The now ancient fortifications were still of some value at this point. A Russian–Ottoman–English alliance led at sea by  Admiral Ushakov and with troops sent by  Ali Pasha retook Corfu in 1799 after a  four-month siege, when the garrison led by general  Louis François Jean Chabot, being short of provisions and having lost the key island of  Vido at the entrance of the port, surrendered and was allowed passage back to France.  [9]  [10]
    According to  Geoffrey Parker in his article,  The Military Revolution 1560–1660: A Myth?, the appearance of the  trace Italienne in early modern Europe, and the difficulty of taking such fortifications, resulted in a profound change in military strategy, most importantly, Parker argued, an increase in army sizes necessary to attack these forts. "Wars became a series of protracted sieges," Parker suggests, and open-pitch battles became "irrelevant" in regions where the  trace Italienne existed. Ultimately, Parker argues, "military geography", in other words, the existence or absence of the  trace Italienne in a given area, shaped military strategy in the  early modern period. This is a profound alteration of the  Military Revolution thesis originally proposed by  Michael Roberts in 1955.
 Parker's emphasis on the fortification as the key element has attracted substantial criticism from some academics, such as John A. Lynn and M. S. Kingra, particularly with respect to the claimed causal link between the new fortress design and increases in army sizes during this period.  [11]
   In the nineteenth century, with the development of more powerful  artillery and explosive shells, star forts were replaced by simpler but more robust  polygonal forts. In the twentieth century, with the development of maneuverable tanks and aerial warfare during and after the First World War, fixed fortifications became and have remained less important than in previous centuries.

note:
尽管社交媒体平台努力铲除世界上最持久的阴谋论，但被称为QAnon的网络现象正在超越其亲特朗普的根源，并迅速蔓延到新的全球社区。
2017年，被称为“Q”的团体或个人发布的隐晦帖子首次出现在4chan的图像板上，传播了一种迅速在网上获得关注的理论，在这场理论中，美国总统正在领导一场与控制国家的“深层国家”的斗争。
今年7月，TikTok屏蔽了几个标签，而Twitter则屏蔽了数千个账户。上个月，Facebook对这场运动发起了全面镇压，包括关闭了790个QAnon相关群组。
但QAnon的追随者们已经迅速适应了他们的新情况，重新塑造了自己的品牌，以避免被发现，并借助于相关的运动来进一步传播他们的信息。
反错误信息组织NewsGuard的欧洲执行主编奇恩·拉布(Chine Labbe)说，“这是一个元阴谋--里面有太多可供选择的东西，这就解释了它为什么会迅速传播。”“它吸引了很多不同圈子和不同意识形态的人。”
Facebook的行动成功地从该平台上删除了一些最大的账户，包括QAnon News and Updates-网站上最大的群体，在巅峰时期拥有20万会员。但自那以后，来自不同社区的一系列团体吸收了新流离失所的信徒。
截至9月9日，在Facebook上搜索“QAnon”，会出现11个成员超过1万人的群，而7月份只有30个。其中一个是名为QAnon Update Group的私人团体，获得了3.9万多个赞，早在QAnon发布第一条帖子之前，之前的名字指的是外星人、流行的视频游戏和一个越南市场。
与此同时，关闭的QAnon小组的成员使用了一系列策略来留在平台上，并继续传播他们的信息。美国媒体事务(Media Matters For America)总裁安杰洛·卡鲁松(Angelo Carusone)表示，一些人已经迁移到其他地区，目标是反面具团体或茶党残余等相关社区，以维持他们在平台上的网络。
“如果(Facebook)在一年前采取行动，你就不会看到(Qanon)从3月份开始的爆炸性增长，”卡鲁松说。“核心基础设施将无法与之同步扩展。”
伦敦智库战略对话研究所(Institute For Strategic Dialog)的研究员Aoife Gallagher补充说，一些QAnon信徒试图逃避侦查，有时是通过改名。一个有大约3000名追随者的团体用单词Cue代替了字母Q。
Facebook的一位发言人表示，该公司总是删除煽动暴力的内容，并已删除了过去违反政策的QAnon账户、群组和页面。他们还表示，他们意识到运动和团体可以快速发展，该平台的团队正在研究避免强制执行的尝试。
社交媒体打压行动出台之际，Qanon已经开始进军一系列新的在线社区，调整其信息，希望扩大其网络，吸引更多追随者。
例如，在Facebook上，与QAnon相关的内容已经发布在一些精神、健康和替代医学小组上。其中一个拥有90,000多名成员的这样的群，以关于水晶治疗、手相和外星人技术的模因为特色，并穿插着与QAnon相关的关于恋童癖和新冠肺炎否认的说法。
“如果你把QAnon表情包发布到这些群组中的一个，它们就可以广泛传播，”卡鲁松说。
反错误信息组织Logical的高级研究员乔·翁德拉克(Joe Ondrak)说，从这些社区中涌现出了一批新的QAnon信徒，他将该组织称为“小写Q”。与阴谋论的政治层面不同，他们主要关注儿童性交易指控-这是QAnon的另一个原始信条。
他说：“它们都与叙事本身有关--除了涉及坏人之外，它们并不注重个性。”“这不是对他们的特朗普崇拜--而是要把孩子们从这个阴暗的阴谋中拯救出来。”
QAnon的内容现在在Facebook上的反虐待儿童小组上自由传播。其中几个是近几个月来创建的，与QAnon推动的关于贩卖儿童的阴谋以及信徒劫持#SavetheChildren等标签的努力不谋而合。
这些团体的内容从恋童癖和虐待儿童的真实新闻报道到错误信息，不一而足。人们经常提到QAnon神话，即使更广泛的运动并不总是明确地命名。“他们把虚构的问题和真实的问题混为一谈，造成了不同叙述的真实纠结，”昂德拉克说。
随着这场运动蔓延到现实世界，理清真实和Qanon叙事的问题变得更加复杂。从逻辑上和渠道，包括NBC文件，QAnon信徒之间的链接和组织反对儿童性虐待的全球游行之间的联系。
翁德拉克辩称，这种较为温和的阴谋形式需要一种特别微妙的政策回应，以遏制虚假信息的传播，同时避免惩罚真正的儿童安全倡导者。
与此同时，Qanon正越来越多地摆脱以美国为中心的业务。拉贝表示：“世界上每个国家都有精英，所以他们设法利用这些当地故事来适应Qanon的框架。”
在法国，QAnon的想法已经与类似模糊的黄色背心运动合并，该运动始于两年前，目的是抗议燃油价格和生活成本。Facebook QAnon群中有三个成员超过1万人，都是法语用户。
根据类似的网络数据，成立于2020年6月的法国阴谋论网站Dissept是主要的QAnon专用网站Qmap.pub的最大转介网站。NewsGuard的一份报告发现，在不到两个月的时间里，它已经通过在线参与达到了法国前2500个网站。
翁德拉克说，在跨越国际边界时，叙事也可能会被大幅改编，有时会以看似矛盾的方式进行改编。他指出，英国的伊斯兰恐惧症团体利用了QAnon的言论，这种情绪在其美国化身中通常不存在。
“这是一种奇怪的延展性和本土化，因为它进一步传播到欧洲，”他说。“他们正在将本土化的偏见折叠成(QAnon信仰)的金字塔。”

note_en:
The online phenomenon known as QAnon is evolving beyond its pro-Trump roots and spreading rapidly into new global communities, despite efforts by social media platforms to stamp out the world’s most persistent conspiracy theory.
 Cryptic posts by the group or individual known as “Q” first began appearing on the imageboard 4chan in 2017, propagating a theory that swiftly gained traction online in which the US president is leading a battle against a “deep state” that wields control over the country.
 In July, TikTok blocked several hashtags, while Twitter banned thousands of accounts. Last month, Facebook launched a sweeping crackdown on the movement, including shutting 790 QAnon-related groups.
 But adherents of QAnon have been swift to adapt to their new conditions, rebranding themselves to avoid detection and piggybacking on to related movements to further propagate their message.
 “It’s a meta-conspiracy—it just has so much in it you can pick and choose from, which explains its rapid spread,” said Chine Labbe, European managing editor at NewsGuard, a counter-misinformation organization. “It seduces people in a lot of different circles and across ideologies.”
  Facebook’s actions succeeded in removing some of the biggest accounts from the platform, including QAnon News and Updates—the largest group on the site, with 200,000 members at its peak. But a range of groups from a wide variety of communities have since absorbed the newly displaced believers.
 As of September 9, a search for “QAnon” on Facebook yielded 11 groups with 10,000 or more members, compared with 30 in July. One, a private group called QAnon Update Group, has more than 39,000 likes and predates the first QAnon post, with previous names referring to extraterrestrials, popular video games and a Vietnamese marketplace.
 Meanwhile members of the closed QAnon groups have used a range of tactics to remain on the platform and continue to spread their message. Some have migrated to other areas, said Angelo Carusone, president of Media Matters for America, targeting related communities such as anti-mask groups or remnants of the Tea Party, to maintain their network on the platform.
 “If [Facebook] had taken their action a year ago, you would not have seen that explosive growth [in QAnon] which began in March,” said Mr. Carusone. “The core infrastructure would not have been able to scale with it.”
 Aoife Gallagher, a researcher at the Institute for Strategic Dialogue, a London-based think-tank, added that some QAnon believers were trying to circumvent detection, sometimes by changing their names. One group with around 3,000 followers had substituted the word Cue for the letter Q.
 A spokesperson for Facebook said that the company always removes content which incites violence, and had removed QAnon accounts, groups and pages that had violated the policies in the past. They also said that they were aware that movements and groups can evolve quickly, and that the platform’s teams were studying attempts to avoid enforcement.
  The social media crackdown came as QAnon had already begun to move into a range of new online communities, tweaking its message in the hope of broadening its network and capturing more adherents.
 On Facebook, for instance, QAnon-related content has been posted on a number of spiritual, wellness and alternative medicine groups. One such group, with over 90,000 members, featured memes about crystal healing, palmistry and alien technology interspersed with QAnon-related claims about pedophilia and Covid-19 denialism.
 “If you post a QAnon meme to one of these groups, they can be distributed far and wide,” said Mr. Carusone.
 A newer breed of QAnon believer has emerged from these communities, said Joe Ondrak, senior researcher at Logically, a counter-misinformation organization, which he dubs “lower-case q.” Rather than the political dimension of the conspiracy theory, they are primarily focused on child sex-trafficking claims—one of the other original tenets of QAnon.
 “They’re all about the narrative itself—they’re not big on personalities except when it comes to the bad guys,” he said. “It’s not the Trump cult for them—it’s about saving kids from this shadowy cabal.”
 QAnon content now circulates freely on anti-child abuse groups on Facebook. Several of these were created in recent months, coinciding with QAnon-driven conspiracies about child trafficking and efforts by believers to hijack hashtags such as #SaveTheChildren.
 Content in these groups ranges from genuine news reports of pedophilia and child abuse to misinformation. References are often made to the QAnon mythos, even when the wider movement is not always explicitly named. “They’re mixing fictional problems with real ones, creating a real knot of different narratives,” said Mr. Ondrak.
 The problem of disentangling real and QAnon narratives has been further complicated as the movement has spilled into the real world. Logically and outlets including NBC document links between QAnon believers and organizing of global marches against child sexual abuse.
 Mr. Ondrak argued that this softer-edged form of the conspiracy requires a particularly nuanced policy response, to counter the spread of disinformation while avoiding penalizing genuine advocates for child safety.
  At the same time, QAnon is increasingly shedding its US-centric focus. “Every country in the world has elites, so they manage to work these local stories to adapt the QAnon framework,” said Ms Labbe.
 In France, QAnon ideas have merged with the similarly nebulous Yellow Vest movement, which began two years ago as a protest against fuel prices and the cost of living. Three of the Facebook QAnon groups with more than 10,000 members are francophone.
 According to Similar Web data, Dissept, a French conspiracy theory website created in June 2020, is the single largest site of referrals to Qmap.pub, the major dedicated QAnon website. A NewsGuard report found that in under two months it had reached the top 2,500 sites in France by online engagement.
 Mr. Ondrak said that narratives could also adapt significantly when crossing international borders, sometimes in seemingly contradictory ways. He pointed to the co-opting of QAnon discourse by Islamophobic groups in the UK, a sentiment not usually present in its US incarnation.
 “It’s this weird malleability and localization as it spreads further into Europe,” he said. “They’re folding localized prejudices into the pyramid of [QAnon beliefs].”

title: QAnon不顾打击，继续在全球范围内无情传播
title_en: Defying crackdowns, QAnon continues its relentless global spread

note:
据俄勒冈州估计，截至周四晚上，俄勒冈州420万居民中有超过10%处于疏散令之下。全球持续升温和极端大风引发的不受控制的野火已经烧毁了大片土地，使该州的空气质量与世界上一些污染最严重的城市不相上下。在撰写本文时，已有多达4万人逃离家园，加入了数以万计的华盛顿州和加利福尼亚州居民的行列，他们所在的州受到历史性野火的威胁。
虽然消防员正在努力控制俄勒冈州数十起野火，官员们表示，其中三起很可能在周五之前合并，但当地警方发现自己正在与虚假信息的洪流作斗争，这些虚假信息的传播速度与大火的传播速度一样快；有传言称，“安提法”或其他出于政治动机的团体正在荒野中漫步，并生火，这是沿海地区一些有组织的袭击的一部分。
Facebook是这些谎言的主要载体。在周四的几个小时内，数以万计的用户分享了所有类似的帖子，声称“协调一致的火灾”是由左翼恐怖分子团伙纵火的。根据联邦调查局的说法，这些说法已经使当地消防和警察机构的资源紧张，他们正在拼命工作，以控制火势。这个谎言的主要传播者在很大程度上依赖于合法的新闻报道，这些报道详细描述了在俄勒冈州、华盛顿州和加利福尼亚州逮捕了几名涉嫌纵火犯-当局声称这些人都没有政治或意识形态动机，也没有彼此之间的联系。
有时被称为虚张声势，巧妙地使用小而真实的陈述来说服人们相信一个更宏大的谎言是一种常见的宣传技巧：在现实中，纵火犯的存在是没有争议的。也就是说，纵火犯不会仅仅因为自然发生的野火而停止纵火。事实上，根据联邦应急管理局的说法，虽然复仇、极端主义和利润都是公认的纵火动机，但其他纵火者寻求“刺激、关注和认可”。更少的纵火者没有犯罪意图，但有临床公认的病理；他们无法抗拒地被迫观察，甚至走向火灾-在某些情况下，他们会纵火。
周四接近午夜时，Facebook表示，其第三方事实核查人员已将有组织攻击的指控评为“虚假”，到那个小时，这些指控已蔓延至数十万用户。这一说法的许多主要传播者向他们的追随者指出了今日执法(LET)的一篇具体文章，该网站将自己描述为“由执法部门拥有和运营”。一位发言人在一封电子邮件中说，Facebook已经采取行动限制这篇文章的覆盖范围。但根据Facebook旗下社交分析平台CrowdTangle的数据，截至周五中午，它仍获得了超过33.8万次互动，其中包括7.1万股。
LET的文章标题为“来源：西海岸的一系列野火可能是‘协调和有计划的’袭击”，显然依靠警方消息来源声称当局已“处于戒备状态”，三个州的纵火事件“可能”是“有组织的袭击”的一部分。文章指出，俄勒冈州和华盛顿州发生纵火的新闻报道，没有一条表明他们之间存在串通。
作者包括以下来源不明的话：“目前有一些担忧和指控说，这些纵火者中的许多人可能与安提法有关。然而，这些指控并未得到(原文如此)证实。“。提交人没有具体说明这些“关切”或“指控”来自谁，虽然他们没有说指控来自他们的执法来源，但他们并没有劝阻读者接受这一概念。除了说这一指控尚未得到证实外，没有人试图对这一指控的真实性进行评级。
在俄勒冈州多达四家警察机构积极恳求公众忽略这一谣言的同时，我们来报道一起有组织袭击的指控。就在几个小时前，道格拉斯县警长办公室开始斥责惊慌失措的居民的非理性行为，这种行为已经开始影响其应急反应：“谣言就像野火一样传播，”警长办公室写道，“现在我们的9-1-1调度员和专业人员正被要求提供信息的要求和对一个不真实传言的询问淹没了，这个不真实的传言称，6名安提法成员因在俄勒冈州道格拉斯县纵火而被捕。”
杰克逊县警长办公室在Facebook上写道：“与杰克逊县灾难性火灾相关的一个日益严重的问题是虚假信息的传播，”并补充说：“请不要基于一些未经证实的随机帖子或表情包来转发和传播错误信息。谣言使保护社区的工作变得更加困难。“。
参议员罗恩·怀登(Ron Wyden)在一封电子邮件中表示：“俄勒冈州的急救人员正在争先恐后地保护俄勒冈州人免受肆虐我们州的野火的伤害。”他说：“他们最不需要的是由虚假的阴谋帖子引发的电话泛滥。Facebook继续行动太慢，无法打击虚假信息的传播，特别是边缘右翼网站和人物的传播。
在推特上，一名独立记者报道称，俄勒冈州莫拉拉的居民在前往该地区拍摄火灾后报了警。根据Twitter上分享的截图，Facebook上一个名为Molalla的私人群组的成员现在据称威胁要“一看到”这个人就开枪。
莫拉拉的另一群记者，包括一名受雇于俄勒冈州公共广播公司(Oregon Public Broadcast)的记者，报道称有三名男子“在民兵风格的检查站”拔枪指着他们。他们说，在被告知“他妈的滚出这里”之前，他们的车牌被拍了下来。
周四晚些时候，“今日执法”对这篇文章做了大量修改，其中包括删除了来源可疑的说法，即对安提法的参与存在“担忧”。标题没有任何变化。“与一直流传的谣言相反，目前没有证据表明野火与极右翼或极左翼活动家有关，”这篇文章的顶部现在写道。同样，还增加了关于紧急情况下谣言影响的警告，这些谣言最初并不存在。
今日执法局全国发言人凯尔·雷耶斯(Kyle Reyes)在一封电子邮件中强调，尽管LET确实援引了一名警方消息人士的话称，火灾可能是一场“协调和有计划的”袭击的一部分，但LET没有将这一说法归因于任何特定的组织或政治意识形态。“[W]我们从未说过火灾是出于政治动机，”雷耶斯在一封电子邮件中说(强调了他的观点)，他在随后的电子邮件中重申了这一点。
“请理解，一项可能是‘协调和计划’的调查并不是指特定的群体--左派或右派。而调查就是这样--调查，收集证据，以某种方式做出决定。分享警报是为了让人们意识到，如果这是一个合法的威胁，他们应该睁大眼睛。“他说(再次强调他的话)。
雷耶斯说，他在波特兰警察局待了四天，昨天才离开该市。他说，联邦执法官员故意泄露信息，让他们正在调查这些所谓的“有计划的袭击”。他说：“他们要求我们让人们知道要保持警惕，警惕任何试图引发火灾的人。”
他继续说：“无论这些罪犯的政治立场如何，多派人盯着生火的人是可以帮助阻止这场灾难的蔓延的。”“在这些艰难的时刻，我们需要集中精力阻止问题……。而不是辩论它背后的政治。每个人的安全和保障都取决于此。“。
加州的红天不仅仅是对我们未来的一瞥--它们是过去的错误最终会发生在我们身上的。
世界末日已经降临到西部风景线上。旧金山湾区笼罩在一层烟雾中，所以…。
多读。
Facebook的一位发言人在一封电子邮件中表示，除了减少对野火谣言负责的帖子的分发外，Facebook还增加了“强烈的警告标签，供看到、试图分享或已经看到的人使用。”我们自己的帖子，获得了近4900次分享，却没有这样的标签。
在后来与Gizmodo的交流中，雷耶斯说他的记者们正在为今天的执法部门撰写一篇报道。他附上了一张记者Twitter账号的截图，当时账号的名字是“安提法居民”，这是特朗普总统在周四的一次竞选活动中发表的关于“安提法”搬进郊区社区的演讲。(“说，亲爱的，谁搬到隔壁来了？‘。‘哦，它是安提法的居民，’“总统说。
来自雷耶斯的问题包括：“你今天疯狂攻击执法部门并试图诋毁我们的消息来源的原因是不是因为你实际上是安提法的自称成员或支持者？”以及“您的编辑或管理团队是否知道您与Antifa的关系？”记者向雷耶斯解释了安提法的笑话，并通知他，莱特可以自由打印他是“安提法”，因此收到的任何死亡威胁都将转发给吉兹莫多的法律顾问。
Gizmodo总编辑John Biggs回答说：“我已经和戴尔谈过关于反法的问题，他承诺不会再这么反法了，”Gizmodo主编约翰·比格斯(John Biggs)回答说。
note_en:
By Thursday evening, more than 10 percent of Oregon’s 4.2 million residents were under an   evacuation order, according to state estimates. Uncontained wildfires fueled by sustained global heating and extreme winds have burnt enormous swaths of land to a crisp, leaving the state’s air quality on par with some of the most prolifically polluted cities in the world. At the time of writing, up to 40,000 people have fled from their homes, joining tens of thousands of Washington and California residents threatened by   historic wildfires in their states.
    While firefighters struggled to contain dozens of Oregon wildfires, three of which officials said were likely to combine by Friday, local police found themselves combating a torrent of disinformation spreading as fast as any blaze; rumors that “Antifa” or other politically motivated groups were roaming the wilderness and lighting fires as part of some coordinated attack along the coast.
 Facebook served as the primary vehicle for these falsehoods. In a matter of hours on Thursday, tens of thousands of users had shared posts all similarly claiming that “coordinated fires” were being set by groups of left-wing terrorists. According to the FBI, these claims have strained the resources of local fire and police agencies working desperately to bring the fires under control. The primary spreaders of this lie have relied heavily on legitimate news reports detailing the arrests of a handful of alleged arsonists in Oregon, Washington, and California—none of whom authorities claim were politically or ideologically motivated or linked to one another.
   Sometimes called    paltering, the artful use of small, truthful statements to convince people of a much grander lie is a common propaganda technique: In reality, the existence of arsonists is non-controversial. To wit, arsonists do not cease committing arson simply because of naturally occurring wildfires. Indeed, while revenge, extremism, and profit are all recognized   motivations for arson, other fire-setters seek “thrills, attention and recognition,” according to FEMA. Even fewer fire-setters demonstrate   no criminal intent, but have a clinically recognized   pathology; they are irresistibly compelled to observe and even travel   toward fires—and in some cases, they start them.
   Close to midnight on Thursday, Facebook said that its third-party fact-checkers had rated as “false” the allegations of coordinated attacks, which had by that hour spread to hundreds of thousands of its users. Many of the primary spreaders of the claim had pointed their followers to a specific article from Law Enforcement Today (LET), a website that describes itself as being “owned and operated by law enforcement.” Facebook had taken action to limit the reach of the article, a spokesperson said in an email. But by noon Friday, it had still garnered more than 338,000 interactions, including 71,000 shares, according to data from Facebook-owned social analytics platform CrowdTangle.
   Titled, “  Source: Series of wildfires on the West Coast may be ‘coordinated and planned’ attack,” the LET article apparently relied on police sources to claim that authorities had been “put on alert” and that incidents of arson in three states “may be” part of a “coordinated attack.” The article pointed to news reports of arson in Oregon and Washington, none of which indicated collusion between them.
 The author included the following unsourced line: “There are current concerns and allegations that many of these people who have started fires may be related to Antifa. However, these allegations have not be [sic] confirmed.” The author does not specify with whom these “concerns” or “allegations” originate, and while they do not say the accusation came from one their law enforcement sources, they do nothing to dissuade readers of the notion. No attempt is made to rate the allegation’s veracity, beyond saying it has yet to be confirmed.
  LET’s reported allegations of a coordinated attack arrived while as many as   four police agencies in Oregon were actively pleading with the public to ignore that rumor specifically. The Douglas County Sheriff’s Office had, just a couple hours before, started   chiding panicky residents over their irrational behavior, which had begun impacting its emergency response: “Rumors spread just like wildfire,” the sheriff’s office wrote, “and now our 9-1-1 dispatchers and professional staff are being overrun with requests for information and inquiries on   an UNTRUE rumor that 6 Antifa members have been arrested for setting fires in DOUGLAS COUNTY, OREGON.”
   “One increasingly problematic issue related to the disastrous fires in Jackson County is the spreading of false information,” the Jackson County Sheriff’s Office   wrote on Facebook, adding: “Please don’t re-post and spread misinformation based on some unverified random post or meme. Rumors make the job of protecting the community more difficult.”
 “Oregon’s first responders are scrambling to keep Oregonians safe from the wildfires ravaging our state,” Senator Ron Wyden said in an email. “The last thing they need is a deluge of calls prompted by spurious conspiracy posts. Facebook continues to move far too slowly to clamp down on the spread of misinformation, particularly by fringe right-wing websites and personalities.”
  On Twitter, one independent journalist reported that residents of Molalla, Oregon, had called the police after they’d traveled to film fires in the area. Members of a private Facebook group called Molalla NOW allegedly threatened to shoot the individual “on sight,” according to screenshots shared on Twitter.
   Another group of journalists in Molalla, including one employed by Oregon Public Broadcasting, reported that three men had pulled guns on them “at a militia style checkpoint.” Their license plates were photographed, they said, before they were told to “get the fuck out of here.”
    Late Thursday evening, Law Enforcement Today made numerous changes to the article, which included removing the dubiously sourced claim that there were “concerns” about Antifa’s involvement. No changes were made to the headline. “Contrary to rumors that have been circulating, there is currently no evidence to tie the wildfires to either far-right or far-left activists,” the top of the article now reads. Warnings about the effects of rumors during an emergency, which were not originally present, were likewise added.
  Kyle Reyes, the national spokesman for Law Enforcement Today, emphasized in an email that while LET did cite a police source claiming fires may be part of a “coordinated and planned” attack, LET did not attribute the claim to any specific group or political ideology. “[W]e  never said the fires were politically motivated,” Reyes said in an email (emphasis his), a point he reiterated in subsequent emails.
 “Please understand that an   investigation that something may be ‘coordinated and planned’ doesn’t reference a specific group—left or right. And an investigation is just that—an investigation, where evidence is gathered and a determination is made one way or the other. The alert was shared to make people aware that they should keep their eyes open in the event that this was a legitimate threat,” he said (again, emphasis his).
  Reyes, who said he had spent four days with the Portland Police Department and only left the city yesterday, said that federal law enforcement officials had purposely leaked to LET that they were investigating these alleged “planned attacks.” “They asked that we let people know to stay alert and keep eyes out for anyone trying to spark fires,” he said.
 “Having additional eyes keeping watch for people lighting fires—regardless of what the politics of those criminals may be—is something that can help stop the spread of this disaster,” he continued. “In these trying times, we need to focus on stopping a problem... not debating the politics behind it. The safety and security of everyone depends on it.”
  California's Red Skies Aren't Just a Glimpse of Our Future—They're Past Mistakes Come to...
 Apocalypse has arrived on the western landscape. The Bay Area is shrouded in a layer of smoke so…
 Read more
 A Facebook spokesperson said in an email that in addition to reducing the distribution of posts responsible for rumors about the wildfires, it had added “strong warning labels for people who see it, try to share it, or already have.” LET’s   own post, which garnered nearly 4,900 shares, contains no such label.
  In a later exchange with Gizmodo, Reyes said that his reporters were working on a story for Law Enforcement Today. He included a screenshot of this reporter’s Twitter account, which at the time bore the name “Resident of Antifa,” a quote by President Trump who during a campaign event Thursday gave a speech about “Antifa” moving into suburban neighborhoods. (“‘Say darling who moved in next door?’ ‘Oh, it’s a   resident of antifa,’” the president said.)
 Questions from Reyes included: “Is the reason you are rabidly attacking Law Enforcement Today and trying to discredit our sources because you in fact are a self-proclaimed member or supporter of Antifa?” and “Are your editorial or management teams aware of your affiliation with Antifa?” This reporter explained the Antifa joke to Reyes and notified him that LET was free to print that he is “Antifa,” and that any death threats received as a result would be forwarded to Gizmodo’s legal counsel.
  “I’ve spoken to Dell about being antifa and he promises to stop being so antifa,” Gizmodo Editor in Chief John Biggs replied.
title: 揭穿“Antifa”野火谣言造成死亡威胁，超载911
title_en: Debunked 'Antifa' Wildfire Rumors Cause Death Threats, Overloaded 911

note:
都在叫我，但我得先买可午餐。很快，我就会前往内华达州的沙漠。我不会孤单一人。现在是大流行前的9月，据报道，数以万计的寻求者正涌向拉斯维加斯以北150英里处的两个没有红绿灯的小镇Hiko和Rachel。这两个地图斑点是距离51区最近的民用前哨。51区是一个戒备森严的军事设施，传说那里有一个机库，里面存放着一艘重力推进的飞船，它可以在星系之间旅行，并通过虫洞，这是基于从外星人那里获得的技术，据一位摇滚明星说，这些技术是从逃往阿根廷的纳粹科学家那里获得的。
为什么每个人都来到一片只适合巨型多毛蝎子居住的土地上呢？就像美国的所有好事一样，这是因为Facebook上的一个表情包。当地人并不觉得好笑。有传言说，定居的人计划点亮他们的财产，并用鸟弹赶走入侵者。从拉斯维加斯出来的15号高速公路上的指示牌警告朝圣者检查他们的轮胎压力和精神状态。内华达州公路巡警说，建议你自己带水、卫生纸，也许还可以多带一罐10加仑的汽油。
我已经做好了适当的准备。我租了一辆SUV庞然大物，因为我要睡在我的车里。我每晚付51美元-明白吗？51区？-一个令人垂涎的沙漠停车场。对我来说，没有怪异的牧场和盘旋的秃鹰。北拉斯维加斯沃尔玛有我需要的一切。我把几磅咸肉、普罗格雷索汤和一个11美元的睡袋塞进我的购物车里，感觉里面装满了石棉。我满载而归，把我的野兽指向北边的Hiko。
我开了两个小时的车，看着我的手机信号褪色，然后就消失了。唉，社区的座右铭“我们并不孤单”被证明是空洞的花言巧语。我完全是一个人。这很奇怪，因为我被告知93号国道会挤满了寻求其他生命形式的同行者，也许是观看不那么专业的运动的更友善的人。
4月27日，美国海军公布了海军飞行员在2004年和2005年在加利福尼亚州和佛罗里达州海岸拍摄的三段视频。视频显示，不明空中现象-新的时髦术语-裸奔在F/A-18超级大黄蜂的雷达屏幕上，而海军飞行员则对着这些神秘的图像大喊大叫。
请注意，海军并没有对这艘飞船是来自火星、伊比沙岛还是天王星发表意见。他们只是正式发布了流传了近18个月的录像，用海军发言人的话说，是为了“澄清公众对一直在流传的录像是否真实，或者这些视频是否还有更多内容的任何误解。”
然后，在6月份，据透露，参议员们已经正式要求五角大楼提供一份关于UFO的全面非机密报告。我已经预料到了这次释放-幸运的是-在大流行时期前不久，我在全国各地奔波，追逐复兴的UFO运动。真相就在那里。
也许吧?。大约33%的美国人认为外星人访问过地球，60%的人认为政府在隐瞒什么，17%的人说他们看到了自己的UFO。那就是5600万美国人。与此同时，美国的教堂出席率下降到了50%，是历史上最低的。这些相应数字的上升和下降并不是巧合。每个人都需要一些可以相信的东西。如果不是J.C.，那还不如是E.T.
赛斯·肖斯塔克(Seth Shostak)是SETI研究所的天文学家，SETI研究所是一个由寻找外星生命的科学家组成的备受尊敬的联盟。肖斯塔克是我所谓的怀疑论者。他相信那里有生命存在，但他认为不明飞行物文化是胡说八道。
“还记得你小时候相信有圣诞老人吗？”肖斯塔克问道。“感觉有比你更伟大的东西在外面控制一切，这感觉很棒。”他对我们的变焦电话一笑置之。“随着年龄的增长，你会意识到，‘嗯，我的父母可能并不总是在这里。’你需要比你更伟大的东西。所以多年来那都是你们当地的礼拜堂。今天，人们在寻找其他东西，外星人填补了这一空白。你会想，‘他们可能会带我去做实验。’“。他又笑了。“‘至少有人对我感兴趣。’”
当我把车开进Hiko时，几乎没有什么活动，只有一些警车在晒太阳。承诺过的Arby的美食车不见了。我进入大本营，开始寻找前MMA大师、现任纪录片制片人杰里米·凯尼恩·洛克耶·科贝尔(Jeremy Kenyon Lockyer Corbell)，他是一位Ufology名人，对他的粉丝来说是一位名人，对他的批评者来说是“他妈的四个名字”
科贝尔在某种程度上是整个骚乱的起因。他最近发布了一部名为“鲍勃·拉扎尔：51区&飞碟”(Bob Lazar：Area 51&amp；Fly Ducers)的纪录片，这部纪录片令人屏息地描述了UFO运动的黑色。这名男子声称，他在20世纪80年代初曾在内华达州的那个地点担任物理学家，在一个机库里遇到了9架外星飞行器。拉扎尔在1989年的最初采访将第51区纳入了美国的意识。唉，他的可信度一直受到质疑，因为他的说法不一致，包括但不限于拉扎尔说他曾就读于麻省理工学院和加州理工大学，而这两所学校没有他的记录。哦，那是一种迎合的信念。
拉扎尔转入地下，在很大程度上被遗忘了，直到科贝尔的纪录片于2019年在Netflix上首映，引起了正反两方面的轰动，一名观众写道，“这部电影纯粹是退缩。”像肖斯塔克这样的专家认为拉扎尔的故事是不明飞行物可信度问题的核心，也就是说，已经有数十万次目击事件，但从来没有任何确凿的证据。肖斯塔克说：“所有这些要么都是轶事，要么依赖于目击者的证词。”“如果量子力学是建立在目击者证词的基础上，你永远不会相信它。”
不管了。纪录片上映后，犹豫不决的拉扎尔·科贝尔在乔·罗根广受欢迎的播客中亮相。数百万人在听，其中包括一个名叫马蒂·罗伯茨(Matty Roberts)的淘气的贝克斯菲尔德孩子，他在Facebook上创建了“暴风雨51区：他们无法阻止我们所有人”的表情包。它在一周内积累了一百万粉丝。罗伯茨建议外星人爱好者9月20日在内华达州会面，冲进基地大门，看看他们能看到什么。
随之而来的在上流社会中被称为“他妈的一群人”。罗伯茨试图与康妮·韦斯特(Connie West)一起组织Alienstock，康妮·韦斯特是基地附近一家汉堡连锁店兼汽车旅馆小A‘Le’Inn的老板。这在Hiko的内华达外星人研究中心引发了一场竞争活动，那里是一个购买星球大战午餐桶和外星人粪便的地方。
在一个没有厕所或附近医院的地方招待一大群露营者的现实吓跑了罗伯茨。他在庆祝活动前一周退出，说他不想在简历上出现Fyre Festival 2.0。剩下的是两个相互竞争的活动，不知道有多少人会参加。
科贝尔站在Hiko人一边，所以这就是我的下场。下车后的五分钟内，我数到了三个绿色的小男人或女人和一位戴着外星人面具的老年绅士，在接下来的36小时里，他会要求我在他的“外星人有权利”请愿书上签名八次。
不难找到科贝尔；他被隔离在一辆曼哈顿公寓大小的房车里，就在舞台后面。一块牌子警告我不要敲门，但我还是要敲门。科贝尔使劲握着我的手，露出夸张的蓝眼睛和超大的瞳孔微笑着。科贝尔身材矮小，留着22世纪的胡须，戴着一顶摇摆者时代的绿色毡帽。
“我很高兴你在这里，伙计，发生了这么多事，”他说。
他的电话坏了。有电话要求查看EDM明星保罗·奥肯福尔德巡演大巴的进展情况-奥肯福尔德前往沙漠进行午夜演出。然后与他的妻子进行了一次悄悄的通话。
“我叫她雪地，因为大多数人从未见过她，怀疑她的存在，”科贝尔说。他的摄影师奈尔斯插话说，“我就叫她‘辣妹’。”
科贝尔想告诉我他是如何来到这里的-这包括在印度的一次濒死经历，巴西的黑魔法武术家，科贝尔拥有的137个网站，以及科学人比尔·奈(Bill Nye)的粗鲁。首先，科贝尔必须和他的妈妈在FaceTime上聊天，他的妈妈已经回到加州的家中。在询问了她儿子的安全后，妈妈一遍又一遍地问着同样的问题：“有人出现了吗？”
科贝尔打开了一个特别装饰的绿色外星人百威啤酒罐头。我问他，他的母亲对她的次子毕生致力于外星人生活有何感想。
“她现在没事了，”他告诉我。“一开始，她只会说，‘你的老二在花生酱里。你他妈的疯了。‘“
这是真的。UFO世界里的每个人都他妈的疯了。事实上，这并不完全正确。UFO世界里的每个人都认为UFO世界里的其他人都他妈的疯了。把ufology想像成基督教的各个教派。他们都信仰同一个神，但天主教徒总是抨击那些总是抨击圣公会教徒的浸礼会教徒。这里说的不是团结这个词。你可以相信，无论我写什么，许多不明飞行物的人都会说它是胡说八道，而我，可能是中情局的一家工厂。
我们从头开始。有些人认为不明飞行物自“旧约”以来就一直伴随着我们。在“以西结”一书中，先知描述了“一辆飞行的战车”，据说这辆战车是由四个天使转动的轮子驱动的。然后先知看到了上帝，描述了他/她，就像，嗯，一个外星人：“我看到，从他的腰部以上，他看起来像发光的金属，好像充满了火，从那里往下看，他像是火；灿烂的光包围着他。”
不是上帝派来的吗？不可知论者认为，如果没有高等生活的帮助，古代苏美尔文明的诞生永远不可能跳到字母表上。如果那是你的包，有历史频道为你准备的节目。
与UFO的可能性并驾齐驱的历史就是UFO诈骗。19世纪，“迪赞之书”在西藏被发现，其中包括一个外星种族在印度定居，试图交朋友，当它变坏时，消失在太空中-但在此之前，它让每个人都失明，污染了空气。
这本书在欧洲引起了不小的轰动。不幸的是，研究人员最终确定，这位名为海伦娜·彼得罗夫娜·布拉瓦茨基(Helena Petrovna Blavatsky)的女士，一位欧洲神秘主义者，实际上是在1890年写下了这篇古文。
欺骗信徒一直是这场运动的中心主题，奥森·威尔斯(Orson Welle)的“世界大战”(War Of The Worlds)就是最好的例证，这是一部声称火星入侵的广播剧，听众纷纷逃往山上。
UFO在20世纪40年代和战后成为一种真实的现象。在战争期间，各方都报告了后来被称为“Foo战斗机”的东西，即跟随空军中队的红色闪烁，并以超高速移动。每个人都认为这是对方新的可怕武器，但事实并非如此，目击事件也从未得到解释。
战后，这场游戏正在进行中。1947年，一架飞机在距离新墨西哥州罗斯威尔陆军机场30英里处坠毁。军方最初称这是一个飞碟，然后改变说法，辩称这是一个实验性的气象气球，但许多人并不买账，开始了长达75年的猜测，认为它是一艘外星飞船。
沙漠成为不明飞行物活动的中心。这似乎很奇怪，因为西南部通常只是你去加州途中要经过的一个地方。也许不是巧合，在20世纪50年代，电影制作人杰克·阿诺德(Jack Arnold)正在拍摄廉价的流行恐怖片，比如它来自荒原上的外太空，把沙漠从沉闷变成了WTF的仙境。
在过去的50年里，这场运动时好时坏。男人和女人被外星人绑架的故事成了一个基本的子体裁。早在20世纪60年代，国会就举行了听证会，空军成立了特别工作组来研究并驳回目击事件。它们被称为“项目标志”、“怨恨项目”和“项目蓝皮书”，是的，“项目蓝皮书”有自己的历史频道节目。
然后是史蒂芬·斯皮尔伯格的“第三种人和外星人的近距离接触”。一本名为“神的战车”的书认为，外星人自文明开始就一直造访地球，尽管遭到历史学家的揭穿，该书还是售出了6700万册。不明飞行物现在正成为主流。九十年代给我们带来了X档案，然后就开始了。
我没怎么注意。不是我的菜。然后，在2017年底，情况发生了变化。我在“纽约时报”上读到大卫·“性”弗雷弗中校的经历。弗雷弗是以尼米兹号航空母舰为基地的F/A 18F超级大黄蜂中队VFA-41的指挥官。2004年11月，尼米兹号在南加州海岸进行训练演习。导弹巡洋舰“普林斯顿”号伴随尼米兹号。一天下午，普林斯顿的雷达开始探测到未知的光点。一名飞行员起飞查看了一下。他只看到了一个巨大的水龙卷，就像你可能看到的船在海洋中倾覆一样。后来，弗雷沃从尼米兹号上起飞，几乎立即开始在他的雷达上看到白色的光点，然后在视觉上看到有什么东西向他的飞机移动。
另一架飞机记录了飞驰的光点的镜头，形状像Tic Tac薄荷糖。Tic Tac以超音速上下移动，左右移动。没有一架飞机像那样飞行。普林斯顿紧张地问弗雷弗，他的喷气式飞机上是否有实弹。(他没有。)。
后来，弗拉沃说，“我不认为这项技术是在这里开发的，我说的‘这里’指的是这个星球。”
海军飞行员是我认识和信任的人。我父亲是海军飞行员。他是从尼米兹号上起飞的。我写了一本关于海军飞行员的书。与大多数不明飞行物目击者不同，他们是训练有素的空中观察员，编造高谈阔论没有任何好处。我开始四处打听。一位有20年航母飞行经验(包括在阿富汗上空旅行)的海军伙伴告诉我，有一次他和他的副驾驶在飞行中看到一个原因不明的超速指示灯。他们互相看了看，没有报告这件事。
“这是一个很小的社区--你不想被视为看到外星人的疯子，”我的朋友告诉我。
弗雷弗的经历证实了这一点。根据一份政府备忘录，飞行员在报告了他们的空中经历后，“详细描述了机组人员经历的高度嘲笑”。(其他飞行员在进入准备室时播放X-Files主题。)。
也许这就是为什么弗拉沃的飞行视频和另外两次遭遇UAP的任务在2017年被解密之前一直被忽视的原因。他们在大张旗鼓的宣传中被释放，并被披露五角大楼已经花费了2200万美元调查UAP。随后是多个播客。
我怎么知道这是个严重的进展？历史频道委托制作了另一个节目。2020年4月，视频的官方“官方”发布带来了又一轮的新闻报道。
所以我去了沙漠。然后去圣地亚哥、拉斯维加斯和其他我不允许提及的地方。我和一位摇滚明星谈到了五角大楼的秘密会议。我在加州星空下吃了些迷幻剂。我几乎在巴斯托的一家自助洗衣店被抢走了所有的衣服。(上一次事件太奇怪了，我不准备详述。)。
就像UFO的经历一样，杰里米·肯扬·洛克耶·科贝尔(Jeremy Kenyon Lockyer Corbell)说的大部分话都必须有信心。他的第三个名字是从他的曾曾叔叔爱德华·洛克耶(Edward Lockyer)那里得到的吗？爱德华·洛克耶在泰坦尼克号上沉没了。是。爱德华是不是爱上了一个头等舱女孩，把她扔进救生艇，弄断了她的腿，然后放下手表，告诉她去找他的母亲，告诉她他爱她？未经核实。他是否参加了加州大学圣克鲁斯分校的九年计划，并成为了MMA大师？是。他有没有创作过一场关于莎伦·泰特的装置艺术展？有记录在案。他是否去过印度，患了一种神秘的发烧，最后从一家将人们扣为人质以榨取保险的凶杀医院逃脱了吗？未经核实。他是不是在巴西跟一位想杀了他的大个子绅士一起训练，结果却被一位巫毒老奶奶善意的干预而幸免于难？天啊，我希望那是真的。
科贝尔对周末寄予厚望，她在房车里蹦蹦跳跳，就像一个三岁孩子扔出的超级球。他的电影今晚将在一个大屏幕上放映，明天鲍勃·拉扎尔将在一个秘密地点进行FaceTime。科贝尔是一位兴奋的主人，他给我做了一个火腿奶酪三明治，但忘了把纸从切达干酪上拿下来。他的狂躁能量是有感染力的，会把你吸引到他计划的任何狂欢中。见面后不久，他宣布我值得信任：“你对我直言不讳，我也会对你直言不讳。酷吗？“。
科贝尔是一个勤劳的人，拥有超凡的魅力，他把圣莫尼卡的一套四人公寓租了出去。(他和雪人一起住在加州沙漠一栋价值100万美元的房子里。)。“我不收任何人的钱，”科贝尔说。“那样的话，就没人能控制你了。”
他的Lazar纪录片以米奇·鲁尔克(Mickey Rourke)为特色，提供了令人费解的叙述，其中包括这样的台词：“记忆是海市蜃楼，是欲望的情妇”和“隐藏的东西是最具诱惑力的，它们挑逗和折磨着我们。”这些台词，加上导致偏头痛的跳跃剪辑，以及科贝尔在明星之间行走的场景，目的是“将你的好奇心武器化”，导致一名观众称这部纪录片“充满了畏缩”。
但是人们看着。这部电影的制作成本约为10万美元，其中7.5万美元花了鲁尔克一个下午的工作时间。“那是朋友折扣，”科贝尔解释说，他在洛尔克在洛杉矶经常光顾的同一家美容院纹身。
科贝尔现年43岁，小时候在硅谷听鲍勃·拉扎尔(Bob Lazar)接受传奇阴谋电台偶像阿特·贝尔(Art Bell)采访时，他的ufology就开始了。这是他毕生的工作，因为他的综合格斗比赛的不幸经历而延误了，但一旦结束，他就弥补了失去的时间。
住在圣塔莫尼卡，他读到过召唤者，男男女女，他们可以用自己的头脑召唤不明飞行物进入他们中间。他开始拍摄召唤者，并将他的视频上传到YouTube上。他的第一个主题是一个无家可归的人。当他回到家时，他看了看他的录像带，--见鬼！--注意到视频上有红色的、飞来飞去的图像。他把它贴了出来，这在洛杉矶成了一件小事，成了当地的新闻。
然后事情变得真实起来。科学人比尔·奈(Bill Nye)称他是骗子，称视频显示的是咸虾：海猴。这激怒了科贝尔。他在接下来的一周里研究了这段视频。最终，他意识到他看到的红色是红牛降落伞小组在附近降落时发出的深红色照明弹。
在那之后，他就上瘾了。最终，他遇到了拉斯维加斯记者乔治·纳普(George Knapp)，后者曾对拉扎尔进行过最初的采访。两人都认识喷气式飞机设计师威廉·李尔(William Lear)的儿子约翰·李尔(John Lear)。
约翰·李尔认为，美国人已经与外星人达成了一项交易，用被绑架者交换外星人的技术，这笔交易的结局很糟糕。六年来，科贝尔每隔一个周末都在拍摄李尔王，直到一位挑衅的李尔王在一张鸡尾酒餐巾纸上签下了他的生命权给了另一位制片人。大约在这个时候，科贝尔开始认为，也许，仅仅是也许，李尔王有点不可靠。那是他开始追求拉扎尔的时候。
我们的谈话被两位客人的到来打断了。首先，这是一个快乐而迷人的戴夫·福利(Dave Foley)，他是音乐厅里的孩子们中的佼佼者。科贝尔和福利是通过共同的熟人乔·罗根认识的，乔·罗根曾与这位加拿大人一起在情景喜剧新闻电台(NewsRadio)中担任主演。Foley明天来参加一个小组讨论，他将为自己作为喜剧演员讲低俗的UFO笑话而道歉。然后门又开了，一位中年母亲和一位迷人的年轻女子走了进来。科贝尔大叫一声：“苏瓦娜！从泰国清迈远道而来。“。
事实上，Suwana是一位闪亮的黑发说唱歌手，来自科罗拉多州，但曾在泰国学习。足够接近
.

are calling me, but first I have to buy Lunchables. Soon, I’ll be heading into the Nevada desert. I will not be alone. It is pre-pandemic September, and tens of thousands of seekers are reported to be descending on Hiko and Rachel, two no-stoplight towns 150 miles north of Las Vegas. The two map specks are the closest civilian outposts to Area 51, a highly guarded military installation where, legend says, a hangar holds a gravity-propelled craft that travels between galaxies and through wormholes based on technology acquired from aliens and, according to one rock star, Nazi scientists who escaped to Argentina.
 Why is everyone descending on a land hospitable only to the giant hairy scorpion? Like all good things in America, it is because of a Facebook meme. The locals were not amused. There are rumors of homesteaders planning to light up their property and shoo off interlopers with birdshot. Signs heading out of Vegas on Highway 15 warn pilgrims to check their tire pressure and sanity. The Nevada Highway Patrol says it is advisable to bring your own water, toilet paper, and maybe an extra 10-gallon jerry can of gas.
  I have made the proper preparations. I rent an SUV behemoth since I’ll be sleeping in my car. I pay $51 a night — get it? Area 51? — for a coveted desert parking lot. No spooky pasture with circling vultures for me. The North Las Vegas Walmart has everything I will need. I pile pounds of salted meat, Progresso soup, and an $11 sleeping bag that feels like it was filled with asbestos into my cart. Loaded up, I point my beast north toward Hiko.
 I drive for two hours, watching my cell signal fade and then vanish. Alas, the   community’s “We are not alone” motto turns out to be empty rhetoric. I am completely alone. This is strange since I was told State Highway 93 would be packed with fellow travelers seeking other life-forms, perhaps kinder ones who watched less professional sports.
 On April 27th, the United States Navy released three videos shot by naval aviators off the coasts of California and Florida in 2004 and 2005. The footage showed unidentified aerial phenomenon — the new hip terms for   —  streaking across the radar screens of F/A-18 Super Hornets while Navy pilots hooped and hollered at the mysterious images.
 Mind you, the Navy wasn’t taking an opinion on whether the craft were from Mars, Ibiza, or Uranus. They were just officially releasing footage that had been circulating for nearly 18 months to, in the words of a Navy spokesperson, “clear up any misconceptions by the public on whether or not the footage that has been circulating was real, or whether or not there is more to the videos.”
  Then, in June, it was revealed that senators had officially requested from the Pentagon a thorough unclassified report on UFOs. I had anticipated the release — got lucky — and, shortly before pandemic times, crisscrossed the nation in pursuit of the resurgent UFO movement. The truth was out there.
 Maybe? About 33 percent of Americans believe aliens have visited Earth, 60 percent think the government is hiding something, and 17 percent say they have seen their very own UFO. That’s 56 million Americans. Meanwhile, church attendance in America is down to 50 percent, the lowest in history. The rise and fall of these corresponding numbers is not a coincidence. Everyone needs something to believe in. If it’s not going to be J.C., it might as well be E.T.
  Seth Shostak is an astronomer with the SETI Institute, a well-respected consortium of scientists looking for alien life. Shostak is what I call a skeptic-believer. He believes there is life out there somewhere, but thinks much of UFO culture is, well, horseshit.
 “Remember when you were little and you believed in Santa Claus?” asks Shostak. “It was great to feel like something bigger than you was out there controlling things.” He laughs over our Zoom call. “You grow older and you realize, ‘Well, my parents might not always be here.’ You need something bigger than you. So for years that would be your local house of worship. Today, people are looking for something else, and aliens fill the bill. You think, ‘They may pick me up for experiment.’” He laughs again. “‘At least somebody’s interested in me.’”
  When I pull into Hiko, there is little activity, just some squad cars bathing in the sun. The promised Arby’s food truck is nowhere to be seen. I enter base camp and begin looking for former MMA guru and  current documentary filmmaker Jeremy Kenyon Lockyer Corbell, a ufologist celebrity to his fans and “fucking four names” to his detractors.
 Corbell is kind of the cause of this entire ruckus. He has recently released a documentary called  Bob Lazar: Area 51 & Flying Saucers, a breathless account of the bête noir of the UFO movement, a man who claimed he had worked as a physicist at the Nevada site in the early 1980s and encountered nine alien craft stowed in a hangar. Lazar’s initial interviews in 1989 had mainlined Area 51 into America’s consciousness. Alas, his credibility has always been in question because of inconsistencies in his story, including but not limited to the fact that Lazar said he went to MIT and Cal Tech and the two schools have no record of him. Oh, and there was that pandering conviction.
 Lazar went underground and was largely forgotten until Corbell’s documentary premiered in 2019 on Netflix, creating a sensation both positive and negative, with one viewer writing, “This movie was pure cringe.” Experts like Shostak put Lazar’s story at the center of the UFO credibility problems, namely there have been hundreds of thousands of sightings and yet there never is any hard evidence. “All of this is either anecdotal or relies on witness testimony,” says Shostak. “You never would have believed quantum mechanics if it was based on witness testimony.”
  No matter. After the documentary’s release, Corbell scored an appearance by the hesitant Lazar on  Joe Rogan’s hugely popular podcast. Millions listened, including a mischievous Bakersfield kid named Matty Roberts, who created the Facebook meme “Storm Area 51: They Can’t Stop All of Us.” It accrued a million followers in a week. Roberts suggested that alien enthusiasts meet in Nevada on September 20th and storm the gates of the base and see what they could see.
 What followed is known in polite company as a clusterfuck. Roberts tried to organize Alienstock with Connie West, the owner of the Little A’Le’Inn, a burger joint and motel near the base. That spawned a competing event at Hiko’s Nevada Alien Research Center, a place to buy  Star Wars lunch pails and alien poop.
 The reality of hosting a horde of campers in a place without toilets or a nearby hospital scared off Roberts. He pulled out a week before the festivities, saying he didn’t want a  Fyre Festival 2.0 on his résumé. What is left are two competing events with no idea of how many people would attend.
 Corbell sided with the Hiko people so that’s where I end up. Within five minutes of exiting my car, I count three little green men or women and an elderly gent in an E.T. mask who, over the next 36 hours, will ask me to sign his “Aliens have rights” petition eight times.
 Corbell isn’t hard to find; he is sequestered in an RV the size of a Manhattan condo just behind the stage. A sign warns me not to knock, but I knock anyway. Corbell shakes my hand hard and smiles with amped-up blue eyes and supersize pupils. A shortish man with 22nd-century facial hair, Corbell sports a  Swingers-era green felt hat.
 “I’m glad you are here, man, so much is happening,” he says.
 His phone is blowing up. There are calls to check on the progress of EDM star Paul Oakenfold’s tour bus — Oakenfold’s heading out to the desert to play a midnight set. Then there is a quiet call with his wife.
 “I call her Yeti because most people have never met her and doubt she exists,” says Corbell. Niles, his cameraman, chimes in, “I just call her ‘hot.’”
 Corbell wants to tell me all about how he had arrived here — it would involve a near-death experience in India, black-magic martial artists in Brazil, 137 Corbell-owned websites, and the rudeness of Bill Nye the Science Guy. First, Corbell has to FaceTime with his mom, who’s back home in California. After inquiring about her boy’s safety, Mom keeps asking the same question over and over: “Did anyone show up?”
      Corbell cracks open a specially decorated green can of alien Budweiser. I ask him what his mom thought of her middle son devoting his life to the pursuit of alien life.
 “She’s OK now,” he tells me. “At first, she’d just say, ‘You’ve got your dick in the peanut butter. You’re fucking nuts.’”
    It’s true. Everyone in the UFO world is fucking nuts. Actually, that is not quite right. Everyone in the UFO world believes everyone else in the UFO world is fucking nuts. Think of ufology like the various sects of Christianity. They all believe in the same deity, but the Catholics are always blasting the Baptists who are always blasting the Episcopalians. Solidarity is not a word spoken here. You can be confident that, no matter what I write, many UFO people will declare it bullshit and me, possibly, a CIA plant.
 We begin at the beginning. Some people think UFOs have been with us since the Old Testament. In Ezekiel, the prophet describes “a flying chariot” that is supposedly powered by wheels turned by four angels. Then the prophet sees God and describes Him/It/Her not unlike, well, an alien: “I saw that from what appeared to be his waist up he looked like glowing metal, as if full of fire, and that from there down he looked like fire; and brilliant light surrounded him.”
 Not a God guy? Agnostics argue that the birth of civilization in ancient Sumeria could never have made the jump to an alphabet without the helping tentacle of higher life. If that’s your bag, there’s a History Channel show for you.
 Walking hand-in-hand through history with the possibility of UFOs has been the UFO fraud. In the 19th century,  The Book of Dyzan was discovered in Tibet and included the story of an alien race that settled in India, tried to make friends, and when it went bad, disappeared into space — but not before blinding everyone and poisoning the air.
 The book created quite the buzz in Europe. Unfortunately, researchers eventually determined the delightfully named  Madame Helena Petrovna Blavatsky, a European mystic, had actually written the ancient text in 1890.
  The duping of believers has been the movement’s central theme, best exemplified by Orson Welles’  War of the Worlds, a radio play purporting a Martian invasion that had listeners skedaddling for the hills.
 The UFO turned into a true phenomenon in the 1940s and postwar era. During the war, all sides reported what came to be called “foo fighters,” flashes of red following air squadrons and moving at superspeed. Everyone assumed it was the other side’s new terrible weapon, but it wasn’t and the sightings were never explained.
 After the war, the game was afoot. In 1947, an aircraft crashed 30 miles away from Roswell Army Air Field in New Mexico. The military initially said it was a flying saucer before changing its story and arguing that it was an experimental weather balloon, but many didn’t buy it, starting 75 years of speculation that it was an alien craft.
 The desert became a center for UFO activity. This seemed weird since the Southwest was usually just a place to get through on your way to California. Perhaps not coincidentally, in the 1950s filmmaker Jack Arnold was setting cheap popular horror films like  It Came From Outer Space in the wastelands, turning the desert from dull to a WTF wonderland.
 The movement has waxed and waned over the past 50 years. Stories of men and women being abducted by aliens became an essential subgenre. Back in the 1960s, Congress held hearings and the Air Force formed special task forces to study and then dismiss sightings. They were called Project Sign, Project Grudge, and Project Blue Book, and, yes, Project Blue Book has its  very own History Channel show.
 Then came Steven Spielberg’s   Close Encounters of the Third Kind and  E.T. A book called  Chariot of the Gods, arguing that aliens had been visiting Earth since the beginning of civilization, sold 67 million copies despite being debunked by historians. UFOs were now mainstreaming. The Nineties brought us  The X-Files, and on it went.
 I didn’t pay much attention. Not my thing. Then, toward the end of 2017, something changed.  I read in  The New York Times about Cmdr. David “Sex” Fravor’s experiences. Fravor was the commanding officer of VFA-41, a F/A 18-F Super Hornet squadron based on the USS Nimitz. In November 2004, the Nimitz was conducting training exercises off the Southern California coast. The guided-missile cruiser USS Princeton accompanied the Nimitz. One afternoon, the Princeton’s radar began picking up unknown blips. One pilot took off and had a look. He saw nothing except a giant waterspout not unlike what you might see if a ship had capsized in the ocean. Later, Fravor launched off the Nimitz and almost immediately began seeing white blips on his radar and then had a visual sighting of something moving toward his plane.
  Another plane recorded footage of the speeding blip that was shaped like a Tic Tac mint. The Tic Tac moved up and down, side to side at supersonic speeds. No plane flew like that. The Princeton nervously asked Fravor if he had live missiles on his jet. (He did not.)
 Later, Fravor remarked, “I don’t think the technology was developed here, and by ‘here,’ I mean this planet.”
 Navy pilots were folks I knew and trusted. My dad was a Navy pilot. He had flown off the Nimitz. I’d written a  book about Navy pilots. They are trained aerial observers unlike most UFO witnesses and have zero to gain by concocting tall tales. I began asking around. A Navy buddy with 20 years of carrier flying, including tours over Afghanistan, told me about a time that he and his co-pilot saw an unexplained speeding light on a flight. They looked at each other and never reported it.
 “It’s a small community — you don’t want to be seen as the nut who saw aliens,” my friend tells me.
 Fravor’s experience confirmed this. According to a government memo, aviators “detailed the high level of ridicule that the aircrew experienced” after reporting their in-air experiences. (Other pilots played  The X-Files theme when they entered the ready room.)
 Maybe that’s why video of Fravor’s flight and two other missions that encountered UAPs were ignored until they were declassified in 2017. They were released to the public amid great fanfare and the revelation that the Pentagon had spent $22 million investigating UAPs. Multiple podcasts followed.
 How did I know this was a serious development? The History Channel commissioned another show. The April 2020 official “official” release of the videos brought another round of press.
 So I headed to the desert. And then to San Diego, Las Vegas, and other places I am not permitted to mention. I talked to a rock star about clandestine Pentagon meetings. I took some hallucinogens under California stars. I nearly had all my clothes snatched at a Barstow laundromat. (That last incident was so weird that I’m not prepared to go into details.)
  Like the UFO experience, much of what Jeremy Kenyon Lockyer Corbell says has to be taken on faith. Did he get his third name from his great-great uncle Edward Lockyer, who went down on the Titanic? Yes. Did Edward fall in love with a first-class girl, toss her into a lifeboat, breaking her legs, then drop down his watch and tell her to find his mother and tell her he loved her? Unverified. Did he go to UC-Santa Cruz on the nine-year plan and become an MMA guru? Yes. Did he create an installation art show about Sharon Tate? Documented. Did he travel to India, fall ill with a mysterious fever, and eventually escape from a homicidal hospital that kept people hostage to milk their insurance? Unverified. Did he train in Brazil with a large gentleman who wanted to kill him only to be spared by the kind intervention of a voodoo granny? God, I want that to be true.
  Corbell has great hopes for the weekend, bouncing around the RV like a Super Ball thrown by a three-year-old. There is going to be a screening of his film on a giant screen tonight, and tomorrow Bob Lazar is FaceTiming in from an undisclosed location. Corbell is an excited host, making me a ham and cheese sandwich, but forgetting to take the paper off of the cheddar. His manic energy is infectious, drawing you into whatever caper he has planned. Shortly after meeting, he proclaimed me trustworthy: “You be straight with me, and I’ll be straight with you. Cool?”
  Corbell is an industrious man blessed with charisma and a fourplex in Santa Monica that he rents out. (He lives with the Yeti in a million-dollar house in the California desert.) “I don’t take money from anyone,” says Corbell. “That way, no one can control you.”
 His Lazar documentary features Mickey Rourke providing indecipherable narration that includes lines like “Memory is a mirage and a mistress to desire” and “Hidden things are the most seductive, they titillate and torment us.” Those lines, along with migraine-inducing jump cuts and a scene of Corbell walking among stars while aiming to “weaponize your curiosity,” led one viewer to call the documentary “full of cringe.”
 But people watched. The film was made for around $100,000, with 75 grand going to Rourke for an afternoon of work. “That was the friend discount,” explains Corbell, who gets his tats at the same parlor that Rourke frequents in Los Angeles.
 Corbell is 43 and got his start in ufology as a boy in the Valley, listening on a clock radio to Bob Lazar being interviewed by legendary conspiracy-radio icon Art Bell. Making it his life’s work was delayed by his MMA misadventures, but once that was over he made up for lost time.
 Living in Santa Monica, he read about summoners, men and women who could conjure UFOs into their midst just with their minds. He started filming summoners and posting his footage on YouTube. His first subject was a homeless man. When he got home, he looked at his footage and —  Holy shit! — noticed red, flitting images descending on the video. He posted it, and it became a minor big thing in L.A., making the local news.
  Then things got real. Bill Nye the Science Guy called him a fraud, saying the footage showed brine shrimp: sea monkeys. That pissed off Corbell. He spent the next week researching the footage. Eventually, he realized the red he was seeing was from crimson flares set off by the Red Bull parachute team descending nearby.
  After that, he was hooked. Eventually, he met Vegas reporter George Knapp, who had done the original Lazar interviews. Both men already knew John Lear, the son of William Lear, the jet designer.
 John Lear believed that the Americans had made a deal with the aliens, exchanging abductees for alien technology, and that the deal had ended badly. Corbell spent every other weekend filming Lear for six years, until a defiant Lear signed his life rights over to another producer on a cocktail napkin. It was around this time that Corbell began to think, maybe, just maybe, Lear was a wee bit unreliable. That’s when he started wooing Lazar.
 Our conversation is interrupted by the arrival of two guests. First, it’s a jovial and charming  Dave Foley, of  Kids in the Hall fame. Corbell and Foley had met through mutual acquaintance Joe Rogan, who had starred with the Canadian on the sitcom  NewsRadio. Foley was here to participate in a panel tomorrow, where he would apologize for making cheap UFO jokes as a comedian. The door then pops open again, and a middle-aged mom and a glamorous young woman step in. Corbell lets out a yelp: “Suwana! All the way from Chiang Mai, Thailand.”
 Actual, Suwana, a shiny brunette rapper, is from Colorado but had once studied in Thailand. Close enough fo
